title: NOC 70010 – Construction managers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Construction Managers (NOC 70010) are the high-level strategists steering Canada through a historic infrastructure and housing boom. As Canada aims to build over 5.8 million new homes by 2030, these managers are the critical link between investment and execution. Unlike site supervisors who handle daily labor, Construction Managers operate at the TEER 0 (Management) level, overseeing multi-million dollar budgets, navigating complex digital twins/BIM software, and ensuring strict adherence to the new 2026 "Green Building" net-zero mandates.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category0 (Management occupations)Major Group70 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportationSub-Major Group700 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportation

duties: Project Lifecycle Management: Planning, organizing, directing, and evaluating construction projects from initial bid to final walkthrough.
Financial Stewardship: Preparing and submitting detailed project budget estimates and monitoring progress against financial milestones.
Contract Negotiation: Negotiating revisions and changes with architects, consultants, suppliers, and subcontractors.
Quality & Safety Control: Developing and implementing rigorous quality control programs and enforcing the latest 2026 safety and building codes.
Stakeholder Representation: Representing the company in union contract negotiations and public business service matters.
employment_requirements: Education: A university degree in civil engineering or a college diploma in construction technology is typically required.
Advanced Credentials: A Master’s degree in Project Management is increasingly required for large-scale infrastructure roles.
Experience: Several years of experience in the construction industry, usually progressing from roles like estimator or site supervisor.
Professional Status: Professional Engineering (P.Eng.) status or Gold Seal Certification from the Canadian Construction Association is highly valued.
salary_prospects: Note: Total compensation often includes significant performance bonuses, vehicle allowances, and profit-sharing.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is rated as Excellent due to the convergence of the housing crisis and a wave of senior-level retirements:
British Columbia: Very Good. Driven by massive civil infrastructure projects and the "Mainland/Southwest" residential boom. BC projects over 9,000 openings in this sector through 2035.

Ontario: Strong Demand. Focused on transit expansion (Metrolinx) and high-density residential towers in the GTA. The province faces a critical shortage of managers capable of handling large-scale ICI (Industrial, Commercial, Institutional) projects.
Alberta & Saskatchewan: Good prospects. Demand is high for Pipeline and Industrial Construction Managers as the energy sector modernizes for hydrogen and carbon-capture projects.
National Trend: 2026 is a "Pivot Year." While material costs fluctuate due to tariffs, the demand for managers who can implement Digital Pour Tracking and Modular Construction is at an all-time high.
Why Work in Canada as a Construction Manager?
High-Stakes Leadership: Manage projects that define the Canadian landscape, from hospitals and schools to renewable energy plants.
Top-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 0 professional, your earning potential is among the highest in the construction sector, often exceeding $115,000+ annually.
Digital Innovation: Lead the transition to "Smart Sites" using AI-driven scheduling, drone-based surveying, and real-time QA/QC workflows.
Immigration Advantage: This NOC is highly competitive in Express Entry general draws and is a priority for many Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker/CEC): As a TEER 0 occupation, Construction Managers are eligible for all primary federal draws. High CRS scores are usually required unless paired with a PNP.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A re-launched stream in 2026 targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Construction Managers with 12 months of Canadian experience are top-priority candidates.
Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Frequently issues targeted invitations for NOC 70010 to support the province's housing and transit goals.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for those with a high-wage offer in the Vancouver or Okanagan-Thompson regions.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Actively recruits managers for the province’s industrial and residential expansion projects.
